title: Ancient Greek-Style Temple
purpose: Render a simplified Doric temple (stepped platform, two colonnades of tapered columns, entablature, triangular pediment) as a reusable architecture scene, bounding against plain sky.
preview_note: octane-preview.png is the native Octane X render (beauty=5000, 96 spp). Re-run via the recipe to reproduce.
native_render_note: Imported OBJ binds a material per usemtl GROUP via assign_material(group_index); binding to the whole mesh does not faithfully colour a combined multi-material subject on this Octane build. The combined-OBJ path (one mesh node, per-group usemtl) is the proven-visible pattern used by octane_visualize_bars and bookshelf-speakers.
quality_checklist: Preview is non-blank and reads as a Greek temple (stepped base, column rows, entablature, pediment) at thumbnail size.
Both usemtl groups (stone, roof) are visually distinct — sandstone body, deeper terracotta pediment.
Scene imports the local scene.obj path listed in commands[].
Camera frames the whole temple with margin (3/4 view).
All 2 usemtl groups in scene.obj have a matching create_material + assign_material(group_index).
If native Octane output differs from intent, record the lesson in docs/recipe-book.md.

required_evidence: bridge result metadata for queued commands
native Octane candidate preview at octane-preview.png
glm-ocr reference and candidate review notes
final native Octane render bundled as octane-preview.png
stop_conditions: candidate content and major silhouettes match the recipe intent (temple: base + columns + entablature + pediment)
task-critical colors/materials are visually distinguishable (stone vs roof)
camera/framing and lighting are close enough for an architecture shot

final_bundle.note: Native Octane X render captured and checked in as octane-preview.png.
